doc.body: Seven red apples and two green apples are in the basket.
doc.question: How many apples are in the basket?

arguments:
<bos><|turn>system
Always end your response with: The answer is <your answer>.<turn|>
<|turn>user
There are 15 trees in the grove. Grove workers will plant trees in the grove today. After they are done, there will be 21 trees. How many trees did the grove workers plant today?<turn|>
<|turn>model
There are 15 trees originally. Then there were 21 trees after some more were planted. So there must have been 21 - 15 = 6. The answer is 6.<turn|>
<|turn>user
If there are 3 cars in the parking lot and 2 more cars arrive, how many cars are in the parking lot?<turn|>
<|turn>model
There are originally 3 cars. 2 more cars arrive. 3 + 2 = 5. The answer is 5.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Leah had 32 chocolates and her sister had 42. If they ate 35, how many pieces do they have left in total?<turn|>
<|turn>model
Originally, Leah had 32 chocolates. Her sister had 42. So in total they had 32 + 42 = 74. After eating 35, they had 74 - 35 = 39. The answer is 39.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Jason had 20 lollipops. He gave Denny some lollipops. Now Jason has 12 lollipops. How many lollipops did Jason give to Denny?<turn|>
<|turn>model
Jason started with 20 lollipops. Then he had 12 after giving some to Denny. So he gave Denny 20 - 12 = 8. The answer is 8.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Shawn has five toys. For Christmas, he got two toys each from his mom and dad. How many toys does he have now?<turn|>
<|turn>model
Shawn started with 5 toys. If he got 2 toys each from his mom and dad, then that is 4 more toys. 5 + 4 = 9. The answer is 9.<turn|>
<|turn>user
There were nine computers in the server room. Five more computers were installed each day, from monday to thursday. How many computers are now in the server room?<turn|>
<|turn>model
There were originally 9 computers. For each of 4 days, 5 more computers were added. So 5 * 4 = 20 computers were added. 9 + 20 is 29. The answer is 29.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Michael had 58 golf balls. On tuesday, he lost 23 golf balls. On wednesday, he lost 2 more. How many golf balls did he have at the end of wednesday?<turn|>
<|turn>model
Michael started with 58 golf balls. After losing 23 on tuesday, he had 58 - 23 = 35. After losing 2 more, he had 35 - 2 = 33 golf balls. The answer is 33.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Olivia has $23. She bought five bagels for $3 each. How much money does she have left?<turn|>
<|turn>model
Olivia had 23 dollars. 5 bagels for 3 dollars each will be 5 x 3 = 15 dollars. So she has 23 - 15 dollars left. 23 - 15 is 8. The answer is 8.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Seven red apples and two green apples are in the basket. How many apples are in the basket?<turn|>
<|turn>model
resps: There are 7 red apples and 2 green apples. To find the total number of apples, you add them together: $7 + 2 = 9$. The answer is 9.

<bos><|turn>system
Always end your response with: The answer is <your answer>.<turn|>
<|turn>user
There are 15 trees in the grove. Grove workers will plant trees in the grove today. After they are done, there will be 21 trees. How many trees did the grove workers plant today?<turn|>
<|turn>model
There are 15 trees originally. Then there were 21 trees after some more were planted. So there must have been 21 - 15 = 6. The answer is 6.<turn|>
<|turn>user
If there are 3 cars in the parking lot and 2 more cars arrive, how many cars are in the parking lot?<turn|>
<|turn>model
There are originally 3 cars. 2 more cars arrive. 3 + 2 = 5. The answer is 5.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Leah had 32 chocolates and her sister had 42. If they ate 35, how many pieces do they have left in total?<turn|>
<|turn>model
Originally, Leah had 32 chocolates. Her sister had 42. So in total they had 32 + 42 = 74. After eating 35, they had 74 - 35 = 39. The answer is 39.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Jason had 20 lollipops. He gave Denny some lollipops. Now Jason has 12 lollipops. How many lollipops did Jason give to Denny?<turn|>
<|turn>model
Jason started with 20 lollipops. Then he had 12 after giving some to Denny. So he gave Denny 20 - 12 = 8. The answer is 8.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Shawn has five toys. For Christmas, he got two toys each from his mom and dad. How many toys does he have now?<turn|>
<|turn>model
Shawn started with 5 toys. If he got 2 toys each from his mom and dad, then that is 4 more toys. 5 + 4 = 9. The answer is 9.<turn|>
<|turn>user
There were nine computers in the server room. Five more computers were installed each day, from monday to thursday. How many computers are now in the server room?<turn|>
<|turn>model
There were originally 9 computers. For each of 4 days, 5 more computers were added. So 5 * 4 = 20 computers were added. 9 + 20 is 29. The answer is 29.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Michael had 58 golf balls. On tuesday, he lost 23 golf balls. On wednesday, he lost 2 more. How many golf balls did he have at the end of wednesday?<turn|>
<|turn>model
Michael started with 58 golf balls. After losing 23 on tuesday, he had 58 - 23 = 35. After losing 2 more, he had 35 - 2 = 33 golf balls. The answer is 33.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Olivia has $23. She bought five bagels for $3 each. How much money does she have left?<turn|>
<|turn>model
Olivia had 23 dollars. 5 bagels for 3 dollars each will be 5 x 3 = 15 dollars. So she has 23 - 15 dollars left. 23 - 15 is 8. The answer is 8.<turn|>
<|turn>user
Mrs. Heine is buying Valentine's Day treats for her 2 dogs. If she wants to buy them 5 heart biscuits each and a set of puppy boots each, how many items will she buy?<turn|>
<|turn>model
resps:
Mrs. Heine has 2 dogs.
For the biscuits, she wants to buy 5 heart biscuits for each dog, so $2 \times 5 = 10$ biscuits.
For the boots, she wants to buy a set of puppy boots for each dog, so $2 \times 1 = 2$ sets of boots.
The total number of items she will buy is $10 + 2 = 12$.

The answer is 12.
